title: Fixing Broken HTML Tags with Missing Closing Tags

statement.markdown:
Given a string containing HTML tags, fix the broken tags by adding missing closing tags. The input string may contain one or more HTML tags, and the broken tags are those without a corresponding closing tag. **Examples** * Input: `

This is a bold

text` Output: `

This is a bold text

` * Input: `
  • Item 1
  • Item 2
` Output: `
  • Item 1
  • Item 2
` **Constraints** 1. The input string contains only alphabetic characters, digits, and special characters (e.g., `<`, `>`, `(`, `)`). 2. The input string contains only valid HTML tags (e.g., `

`, ``, `

    `, `
  • `). 3. The input string contains at most 10,000 characters. 4. The input string contains at least 1 character.
statement.inputFormat: A string containing one or more HTML tags.
statement.outputFormat: The input string with missing closing tags added.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input string is well-formed and does not contain any invalid HTML tags.

constraints.description: Maximum length of the input string

examples.explanation: The missing closing tag is added to the input string
The missing closing tag is added to the input string

hints.text: Start by parsing the input string to find all HTML tags.
Create a stack to keep track of opening tags and add the corresponding closing tags.

editorial.title: Fixing Broken HTML Tags with Missing Closing Tags
editorial.intuition: Use a stack to keep track of opening tags and add the corresponding closing tags.
editorial.bruteForce: Try to parse the input string and manually add closing tags.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a stack to keep track of opening tags and add the corresponding closing tags.
editorial.optimalSolution: Create a stack and iterate over the input string. When an opening tag is found, push it onto the stack. When a closing tag is found, pop the corresponding opening tag from the stack and add the closing tag.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The stack ensures that every opening tag has a corresponding closing tag.
editorial.dryRun:
Start with the input string '

This is a bold

text' and iterate over it using the stack.
commonMistakes: Trying to add closing tags for non-opening tags
